title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed three RBIs in the Red Sox's win against Cleveland, despite his reduced role as catcher.
Wong, once a primary catcher, has fallen below Mickey Gasper and Carlos Narvaez in Boston's catching hierarchy.
Wong's .245 average this season may position him as Red Sox's secondary catcher over Narvaez with a .210 average.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings with seven strikeouts in the Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray has a 3.06 ERA for the season and recorded a 2.00 ERA in five May starts.
The Red Sox are 7-3 in Gray's starts, and he holds a $30 million option for 2027.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester features Boston sports stars and celebrities.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels lead Team Patriots and Team Sox, respectively.
Event supports MayeDay Foundation, aiding children and families in need; doors open at 5 p.m. Sunday.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots are reportedly close to acquiring Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown with a trade framework in place.
A potential trade post-June 1 would reduce the Eagles' salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
The deal may hinge on the Patriots agreeing to trade a first-round pick, which they currently resist.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom heard in Greater Boston was likely caused by a bolide meteor reentry, not an earthquake.
Meteorologists used satellite data to identify a large flash east of Boston, indicating a meteor event.
Massachusetts public safety officials reported no linked emergencies despite ground tremors and 911 calls.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox dropped to 23-33, last in the AL East, losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and TD Garden for their passion during a recent livestream.
Despite rumors, Boston is not the favorite to land Antetokounmpo; Miami Heat currently have an edge in trade talks.
Speculation surrounds a potential trade involving Jaylen Brown as part of a deal to acquire Antetokounmpo.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed Harvard's Class of 2026 Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge in Cambridge citing crowd concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded at 5 a.m. to reports of crowd blocking and attempting to swim in the Charles River.
No arrests or injuries were reported immediately; Cambridge and Harvard police also assisted in clearing the area.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Four Seasons' Season 2 featuring Tina Fey and Will Forte is now streaming on Netflix.
'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is available to stream on Peacock.
'Star City,' a spin-off of 'For All Mankind,' is streaming on Apple TV+.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are predicted to draft Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, at 6'5" and 200 pounds, has been compared to MLB's Freddie Freeman by multiple scouts.
Lowrance, committed to the University of Virginia, is noted for his hitting ability but may switch from third to first base.
